title: dysoxylum ficiforme
intro: Dysoxylum ficiforme, also known as East Indian rosewood or Indian rosewood, is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Dysoxylum ficiforme include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and inflammation, and support digestive health. Dysoxylum ficiforme is traditionally prepared as a decoction, tincture, or powder, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
